hook: Your state has a will for you. You didn't write it.
body: If you pass without a will, your state's intestacy laws decide who gets what. That might not match what you'd choose. A will is your voice when you're no longer here to speak.

hook: When a parent dies without a plan, families pay the price.
body: Frozen accounts, missing documents, and family disagreements are the reality when parents leave no estate plan behind. You can't go back and fix it after the fact. Starting the conversation now — even a simple one — changes everything.

hook: A judge shouldn't decide who raises your children.
body: Without a named guardian in your will, that's exactly what happens. The court picks someone — based on law, not love. Naming a guardian is one of the simplest, most important things a parent can do.

hook: Probate can freeze your family out for years. Plan around it.
body: When an estate goes through probate, your family waits — sometimes for months, sometimes longer — while courts sort out what you owned and who gets it. The right planning documents can help your estate skip that process entirely. It starts with knowing what you have and what's missing.

hook: No power of attorney means no legal authority to help your parent.
body: If your parent becomes incapacitated without signing a power of attorney, you can't manage their finances or make decisions for them — not without going to court first. It's one of the most overlooked documents in estate planning, and one of the most important. The time to get it signed is now, while they're well.

hook: Your family's bills don't stop when your paycheck does.
body: Life insurance is one of the most important tools a young family has — but only if it's set up correctly. Wrong beneficiary designations or coverage gaps can leave your family in a difficult spot. Make sure your policy is part of a plan, not just a checkbox.

hook: Trusts aren't just for the wealthy. They're for the prepared.
body: If you own property or retirement accounts, a trust can help your estate bypass probate and land exactly where you want it — on your timeline. As retirement tax strategies grow more complex, how you hold your assets matters just as much as how much you have. Find out if a trust belongs in your plan.
